filled.patient_occupation: Senior carer at a residential dementia unit (private healthcare)
filled.incident_description: The claimant was the driver of a Range Rover, travelling at approximately ten miles per hour as she approached a roundabout in Scotland. Another vehicle collided with the rear of her vehicle at speed, with the front of the other vehicle going partially underneath the claimant's car owing to its higher ride height. The claimant was jolted forward upon impact. The airbags did not deploy. Following the collision, the claimant was unable to drive her vehicle and a bystander moved it for her. The other driver initially left the scene but was subsequently located by police at a nearby retail park.
filled.immediate_medical_attendance: The claimant did not attend hospital immediately following the incident. The police at the scene noted that the claimant was walking abnormally and offered to take her to hospital; however, the claimant declined as she had her grandson with her. She took a taxi back to her daughter's residence in Scotland.
filled.subsequent_medical_attendance: The claimant attended her GP approximately two days after the incident, upon returning home from Scotland. The GP advised that the insurance company should manage her treatment. The claimant returned to her GP the following day, where a physiotherapist examined her and similarly recommended that she await treatment arranged through her insurance. The claimant subsequently underwent ten weeks of physiotherapy and acupuncture, arranged through her insurance provider. She also took ibuprofen and paracetamol for the initial few days following the incident.
physical_symptoms: Neck pain: Onset was practically immediate on the day of the incident. Initially described as mild in severity with associated stiffness. Currently largely resolved to normal but with intermittent exacerbations upon sudden movement.

Lower back pain: Onset on the day of the incident. Initially described as moderate in severity with stiffness. Currently largely resolved to normal but with intermittent twinges in the midline lumbar region. The claimant has been using kinesiology tape for support.
Left shoulder pain: Onset on the day of the incident. Initially described as mild in severity. Currently largely resolved but with intermittent twinges, particularly on turning movements.
Left arm pain: Onset on the day of the incident. Initially described as moderate in severity. Currently resolved to normal.

Bilateral hand and thumb pain: Onset the day after the incident. Initially described as moderate in severity with a bruised sensation. The right hand has resolved. The left thumb remains symptomatic with soreness along the extensor pollicis longus (EPL) tendon, suggestive of possible tendonitis.
filled.psychological_symptoms: The claimant reports significant travel anxiety following the incident. She has been unable to drive back to Scotland since the accident and has not seen her grandson since December. She was unable to drive herself to the consultation and required someone else to drive her. She reports being nervous driving anywhere that is not local to her home and restricts her driving to the three-mile journey to and from work. She reports experiencing sweating whilst in the car. The examining doctor has recommended a referral to a psychologist for further assessment and treatment.
filled.pre_existing_conditions: The claimant has a past medical history of chronic kidney disease stage four, with a current glomerular filtration rate of approximately twenty percent. She underwent a left nephrectomy approximately fifteen years ago following a renal tumour, and the remaining kidney has deteriorated over time. She is currently on the transplant waiting list and is under regular review. No other relevant past medical history was reported.
filled.medical_history: Chronic kidney disease stage four. Left nephrectomy approximately fifteen years ago secondary to a renal tumour. Currently on the renal transplant waiting list and under regular review.
filled.past_accidents: The claimant reported that she has never previously been involved in a road traffic accident.
filled.living_arrangements: The claimant reports no change to her living arrangements as a result of the incident. She continues to reside in the same property and sleep in the same bed.
filled.effect_on_work: The claimant was placed on light duties at work for just over two weeks following the incident, during which time she did not undertake any lifting. She continued to carry out medication administration duties during this period. She subsequently returned to her normal duties on a gradual basis.
filled.effect_on_life: The claimant reports a significant impact upon her family life. She has been unable to drive to Scotland to visit her daughter and grandson since the incident and has consequently missed family occasions, including her daughter's and son's birthdays. She reports being unable to drive anywhere beyond her local area, restricting her driving to the three-mile commute to and from work. She required someone else to drive her to the medico-legal consultation.
examination_findings: Cervical spine: Flexion, extension, and rotation were assessed. The claimant demonstrated some stiffness on forward flexion of the neck. Lateral rotation was achievable but with reported twinges.
Left shoulder: Range of movement assessed including abduction and adduction. Movements were noted to be slightly stiff.
Left arm: Movements assessed and found to be satisfactory.

Lumbar spine: Tenderness noted on palpation in the midline of the lower back. The claimant was apprehensive regarding flexion and extension owing to fear of pain recurrence, with associated stiffness noted.

Hands: Bilateral hand movements were assessed and found to be satisfactory. The claimant reported soreness along the extensor pollicis longus tendon of the left thumb on movement, suggestive of possible tendonitis. Grip strength and thumb opposition appeared intact.
filled.rehabilitative_treatment: The examining doctor recommends further physiotherapy for the claimant's left shoulder stiffness and lower back stiffness. A referral to a psychologist is also recommended for assessment and treatment of the claimant's travel anxiety and driving-related psychological symptoms.
filled.diagnosis_and_prognosis: The claimant sustained soft tissue injuries to the neck, lower back, left shoulder, left arm, and bilateral hands (including thumbs) as a result of a rear-end road traffic collision on 04/12/2023. The neck pain was initially mild and has largely resolved, though the claimant experiences intermittent exacerbations on sudden movement. The lower back pain was initially moderate and has largely resolved, though intermittent twinges persist in the midline lumbar region with associated stiffness. The left shoulder pain was initially mild and has largely resolved, though intermittent twinges remain, and examination revealed some residual stiffness. The left arm pain was initially moderate and has fully resolved. The bilateral hand and thumb pain was initially moderate; the right hand has fully resolved, but the claimant continues to experience soreness in the left thumb along the EPL tendon, possibly consistent with tendonitis. The claimant also reports significant psychological symptoms, including travel anxiety, an inability to drive beyond her local area, and avoidance of driving to Scotland. Further physiotherapy is recommended for the residual left shoulder and lower back symptoms, and a psychology referral is recommended for the travel anxiety. With appropriate treatment, the remaining physical symptoms are expected to continue to improve. The psychological symptoms require specialist assessment to determine prognosis.
